rootCause.summary: User submitted incorrect password due to an accidental extra character, leading to a failed login without visible error feedback.
triggerChain: User typed 'abc' then 'def' into username/password (initial incorrect attempt)
User copied 'tomsmith' from the page's suggested credentials
User pasted 'tomsmith' into the username field
User copied 'SuperSecretPassword!' from the page's suggested credentials
User pasted 'SuperSecretPassword!' into the password field
User accidentally typed '1' into the password field after pasting, changing the password to 'SuperSecretPassword!1'
Form submitted with incorrect password to /authenticate endpoint
Application rejected login and remained on the login page without displaying an explicit error message or navigating away.
evidence: Click event on `EM` with text 'tomsmith' at 1775981462246 (copy username)
Keyboard events for `ControlLeft` and `KeyV` targeting `INPUT#username` at 1775981465568, 1775981465732 (paste username)
Click event on `EM` with text 'SuperSecretPassword!' at 1775981468560 (copy password)
Keyboard events for `ControlLeft` and `KeyV` targeting `INPUT#password` at 1775981470861, 1775981471015 (paste password)
Keyboard event `Digit1` targeting `INPUT#password` at 1775981472056 (accidental extra character '1')
The `pageUrl` remains `https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login` at the end of the recording, indicating no successful navigation after login attempt.
Absence of any visible error message or UI change in the recording after form submission.
rootCause.fix: Implement clear, visible, and user-friendly error messages on the login page for failed authentication attempts (e.g., 'Invalid username or password'). Consider adding a 'show password' toggle to help users verify their input, especially when pasting.

bugReport.title: Login fails silently due to incorrect password, no error message displayed
bugReport.description: The user attempted to log into the application. They correctly copied and pasted the username 'tomsmith' and the password 'SuperSecretPassword!'. However, after pasting the password, the user inadvertently typed an additional character '1'. Upon submitting the login form, the authentication failed as expected due to the incorrect password ('SuperSecretPassword!1'). The application, however, did not provide any visual feedback (e.g., an error message) on the login page, nor did it navigate away. This lack of feedback leaves the user unaware of the reason for the failed login.
timeline: 1775981453474: User clicked on the username input field.
1775981453917-1775981455872: User initially typed 'abc' into username and 'def' into password fields.
1775981465498: User clicked on the username input field.
1775981465732: User pasted 'tomsmith' into the username field.
1775981470698: User clicked on the password input field.
1775981471015: User pasted 'SuperSecretPassword!' into the password field.
1775981472056: User typed '1' into the password field, making the input 'SuperSecretPassword!1'.
End of recording: User remained on the login page; no error message was visible, and no navigation occurred.
networkActivity: GET https://298279967.log.optimizely.com/... (Optimizely log) failed with status 0 (Network error) at 1775981462078. This is likely unrelated to the core login functionality.
POST https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/authenticate was submitted, but its network response (status code, body) is not captured in the recording.
bugReport.consoleLogs: No relevant console errors or warnings were recorded.

environment.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/146.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

steps: Copy the username 'tomsmith' from the text provided on the page (e.g., 'Your username is tomsmith').
Copy the password 'SuperSecretPassword!' from the text provided on the page (e.g., 'Your password is SuperSecretPassword!').
Immediately after pasting the password, type the character '1' into the Password field.
reproductionSteps.expectedBehavior: Upon clicking 'Login', the application should display a clear error message on the login page (e.g., 'Invalid username or password') indicating that the authentication failed due to incorrect credentials. The network request for `POST /authenticate` should return a non-2xx status code (e.g., 401 Unauthorized) or a 200 OK with an error message in the response body, and the page should update to show this error.
reproductionSteps.actualBehavior: The login attempt failed, and the user remained on the login page. No visible error message was displayed on the page, and there was no navigation to a different page. The network response for the `POST /authenticate` call was not captured in the recording.
